creator: flz@xbsd.org
text:
On Jun 2, 2005, at 2:11 PM, Dag-Erling Sm=F8rgrav wrote:

> Florent Thoumie <flz@xbsd.org> writes:
>
>> On Jun 2, 2005, at 12:13 PM, Dag-Erling Sm=F8rgrav wrote:
>>
>>> The PR was submitted at a time when doscmd was part of the base
>>> system.  While it no longer is in the base system, it is still
>>> maintained in our CVS tree, and I see no reason to close the PR.
>>>
>> Well, GNATS is commonly used for ports-related problems, not really
>> for software-related problems, being in projcvs or not.
>>
>
> Excuse me?  Gnats is used to report bugs anywhere in any of our CVS
> repositories.  If you object to the classification of this PR as a
> ports PR, feel free to move it to a different category, but do not
> close it.  Please read our PR handling guidelines (and take note of
> who wrote them in the first place)

Ok, I mis-talked. I meant ports category should be used for ports-=20
related
problems, this is a port-related problem because it doesn't show that
it's broken without X support. If this is to be corrected, this can
be moved to another category like you suggested. Sorry for the
incomprehension.

--=20
Florent Thoumie
flz@xbsd.org
